releases.markdown:
# CMS v1.15.0

_2026-06-29 · changes since r-1.14.0_

## Features
- Page builder: per-field translation in the SEO/settings forms, so individual fields can be translated independently.
- Page builder: a gallery image picker for selecting media-library assets into fields.
- A configurable translation model setting (per-site translate-model), feeding the translate flow.

## Other
- Internal refactor: the page-builder shell and the render pipeline were split into smaller per-component / per-concern files (plan-tree, plan-section, plan-list, etc.), plus render hardening. No user-facing change from the refactor itself.

# CMS v1.14.0

_2026-06-29 · changes since r-1.13.1_

## Features
- Page builder: sections now have editable, inline-renamable names (auto-defaulting to "Section N").
- Chat: type `@` in the composer to autocomplete the current page's sections; mentioning a section feeds its full resolved contents to the assistant so it targets the exact block instead of guessing.
- Media gallery: tag your assets, search by tag, and edit tags inline from the gallery tiles.
- Media: when attaching an image to chat, a downscaled thumbnail is sent to the assistant for description while the full file still uploads.
- Chat polish: inline confirm before deleting a saved thread, a Retry affordance on failed turns, a single-line status footer with Debug moved to an overflow menu, and attached/referenced images shown inline as thumbnails in the message bubble.

## Fixes
- Chat: HTML comments and whitespace-only assistant parts no longer render as empty bubbles; long lines wrap in the code editor; tool-call pills are slimmer and wrap per row.
- Component develop view no longer causes a hydration mismatch (persisted view is read in an effect, not during render).
- Render: label expressions handle surrounding backticks automatically (legacy values heal on render, no migration needed).

# CMS v1.13.1

_2026-06-26 · changes since r-1.13.0_

## Fixes
- Chat panel no longer grows off the top of the screen when resized — its max height now fits the space above the bottom anchor, so the input and latest messages stay visible.

# CMS v1.12.3

_2026-06-26 · changes since r-1.12.2_

## Other
- No CMS-facing changes. This release accompanies deployer-side updates; the CMS bundle is unchanged from r-1.12.2.

# CMS v1.11.0

_2026-06-26 · changes since r-1.10.5_

## Features
- Chat: a gallery picker lets you attach existing media-library assets to a chat message, alongside upload and drag-and-drop.

# CMS v0.11.0

_2026-06-24 · changes since cms-v0.10.0_

## Features
- Collections: import and export items as CSV or JSON, from a new operator UI.
- Collections: a schema manager to add, drop, and rename fields, with atomic live-store rebuilds.
- Collections: AI tools to drop and rename collection fields (schema evolution).
- Collections: an operator raw-SELECT SQL console (fenced SELECT-only, not exposed to the AI).
- AI chat panel resizes via a top-left pointer-drag handle (replacing the native CSS resize).
- Chat: scroll-to-latest pill with follow-bottom-only transcript behavior.
- Chat: tool cards get a show-more toggle for truncated input/output blobs.
- Chat: inline active-override label near the input (PM-SSO).
- Accessibility: focus trap and initial focus in the open chat dialog, Esc minimizes the panel, and focus-visible rings on widget buttons.

## Fixes
- Collections: fix collection-create failing when the CREATE TABLE spanned multiple lines (use prepared statement instead of D1 exec).
- Collections: restore the missing Collections nav-item icon.
- Chat: fix the model-picker crash on stale-cache rows from /api/chat/models.
- Chat: fix the expand/shrink toggle getting stuck one-way (toggle on size, not preset).

# CMS v0.10.0

_2026-06-24 · changes since cms-v0.9.2_

## Features
- AI chat assistant panel is now resizable, with default/half-screen preset toggles.
- Chat input is a resizable textarea with a switch for Enter behavior (send vs. newline).
- The model picker shows per-model prices (input/output USD per 1M tokens) and input-modality icons, and is filtered to tool-call-capable models only.
- Your selected model now persists across reloads (validated against the live catalog).
- The chat widget shows the remaining credit on the in-use OpenRouter key.
- Tool calls are persisted in chat history and rendered as cards (name shown once, with an input/output accordion).
- The launcher shows an unread badge when minimized and a new reply arrives.
- PM-SSO operators can export a chat as the full model-payload JSON.
- PM-SSO operators can edit system-prompt version overrides from the debug panel, backed by a new prompt-version store and gated prompts API.

## Fixes
- Accept 48-byte `CMS_AUTH_SECRET` values: the AES key is now SHA-256-derived instead of being rejected by the secret-box KEK.

# CMS v0.9.2

_2026-06-24 · changes since cms-v0.9.1_

## Fixes
- **"Sign in with BizBeeCMS" SSO button now always shows on the admin login.** It was hidden unless you arrived from ProjectManager via a specific link, so opening `/admin` directly (or after an apex→www redirect) showed only email/password. The button now appears whenever the Site is wired to ProjectManager.

# CMS v0.8.0

_2026-06-23 · changes since cms-v0.7.0_

## Features

### Per-site OpenRouter API key (new)
- Each site can now use its **own** OpenRouter API key for the AI assistant, set in ProjectManager → Site settings (write-only field: shows "key set" / "no key", never reveals the stored value, with a "Clear key" action).
- The key is **encrypted at rest** in ProjectManager (AES-256-GCM), decrypted only at deploy time, sent to the deployer over HTTPS, and installed as a real **secret** on that site's CMS Worker — never a plaintext variable, never written to a deploy log.
- Graceful fallback at every step: a site with no key (or a key that can't be decrypted) falls back to the deployer's global OpenRouter key, and if that's also unset, to the built-in Workers-AI provider. A missing key never breaks a deploy.

## Other
- New ProjectManager secret `SITE_SECRET_KEY` (the encryption key) and a `sites.openrouterApiKeyEncrypted` column + migration.
- The deployer now sets the CMS Worker's OpenRouter key via `wrangler secret put` instead of a build-time variable.

# CMS v0.7.0

_2026-06-22 · changes since cms-v0.6.0_

## Features

### Content collections (new)
- Define your own data collections — each becomes a real database table, created safely behind a hardened SQL fence (no raw SQL ever reaches the table layer).
- Full admin UI: create and edit a collection's schema with a field-type picker, then manage its items in a table with the right input per type (date/time, number, select, boolean, multiselect, rich text).
- Structured search, filter, sort and pagination over collection items.
- Bind collection data into pages: a single block can pull one record's fields into its props, or a new built-in **List** block can stamp a component once per matching record — all authored visually in the page builder, with graceful empty states.
- AI assistant can create collections, add/update/archive items, query data, and wire up component bindings — all through structured tools, never raw SQL.

### CMS-local accounts & roles (new)
- Sign in directly in the CMS with email + password, **Sign in with Google**, or PM single-sign-on — no more forced redirect to ProjectManager.
- Four-role hierarchy (SuperAdmin / Admin / Manager / Editor) with server-enforced permissions on every admin route.
- Invite teammates by email (sent via Cloudflare Email) with a role-limited invite form; accept-invite flow creates the account and signs them in.
- User-management screen: list users and pending invites, change roles, and remove users — all gated by who's allowed to do what.

### Manage a site from your own AI tools (new)
- Each deployed CMS exposes a remote MCP server at `/mcp`, so you can connect Claude Code (or any MCP client) and manage the site with your own model.
- Mint and revoke per-site API keys in **Settings → API Keys**, with a copy-paste "Connect Claude Code" snippet.

### Component kits
- Tag components, then export everything with a tag as a single installable kit bundle — and import a kit in one step.

### AI provider
- The AI assistant now runs on OpenRouter by default (with the built-in Workers-AI provider as fallback), and its model picker lists the full OpenRouter catalog.

## Other
- Hardened the runtime SQL fence with a parsing validator and an attack-string test corpus; added a schema-rebuild planner for future field drop/rename.
- Numerous database migrations for the new auth, roles, tags, API-key, invite and collection tables (applied automatically on deploy).
- Trilingual (EN/FI/ET) coverage for every new screen.
